title: Photos: State trooper killed by wrong-way driver in Lynnfield crash laid to rest
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Trooper Kevin Trainor, 30, was honored at a funeral service in Salem, Massachusetts on May 13, 2026.
Trainor was killed by a wrong-way driver, Hernan Marrero, on Route 1 in Lynnfield on May 6, 2026.
Massachusetts officials, including Gov. Maura Healey, attended the service at St. James Church.

title: Angell Animal Medical Center treating cat who fell 10 stories
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A cat named Mazy survived a ten-story fall in the Greater Boston area and is treated at Angell Animal Medical Center.
Mazy has surgeries scheduled to repair broken paws and a collapsed lung, with medical costs estimated at $15,000.
MSPCA-Angell investigates the cause of the cat's fall; donations are sought to cover medical expenses.

title: AG says auditor can hire attorney to force audit of Legislature after SJC order
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Attorney General Andrea Campbell approves Auditor Diana DiZoglio's request to hire an attorney for a legislative audit.
Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court orders AG to decide on auditor's representation request within 30 days.
Attorney Shannon Liss-Riordan will represent the audit focused on Senate finances from fiscal years 2021-2024.

title: Kyle Schwarber reveals tepid pursuit from Red Sox this past offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kyle Schwarber had minimal offseason contract talks with the Red Sox before re-signing with the Phillies in December 2025.
Since leaving Boston, Schwarber has hit 204 home runs for the Phillies, thriving as a top MLB slugger.
Schwarber's career OPS at Fenway Park ranks fifth all-time, showing his consistent success at the venue.

title: Jayson Tatum reveals new goal he’s chasing with the Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum aims to win another championship and the NBA Finals MVP with the Celtics in the 2026-27 season.
Tatum missed much of the 2025-26 season due to a ruptured Achilles, but returned to play by March.
Tatum averaged 21.8 points, 10 rebounds, and 5.3 assists per game post-injury, despite knee issues in playoffs.

title: 7 contracts Bruins might try to unload this offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins aim to free up cap space, considering moving Joonas Korpisalo and his $3 million contract.
Defenseman Henri Jokiharju's $3 million deal might be shed, given limited playing time and performance issues.
Versatile forward Elias Lindholm's $7.75 million contract presents a challenge due to his performance and injury history.

title: First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Massachusetts coast
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A dead gray seal on Martha's Vineyard with shark bite wounds confirmed first 2026 white shark sighting.
New England Aquarium advised beachgoers to be cautious as white shark numbers increase through the summer.
White shark sightings aid research and public safety, report sightings via Atlantic White Shark Conservancy’s Sharktivity app.

title: 10 stats to help contextualize the Red Sox’ rocky start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox are 0-21 when trailing after seven innings, indicating late-game struggles.
Team ranks 29th in MLB with only 29 home runs, highlighting offensive deficiencies.
Red Sox excel defensively with 41 defensive runs saved, leading the majors in this category.

title: Want to own a piece of Dunkin’? Its parent company may go public.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Inspire Brands, parent of Dunkin', plans to go public, marking its third potential IPO in eight decades.
The company owns other chains like Arby's and Sonic and has filed confidentially for an IPO with the SEC.
Proceeds from the IPO are intended to repay Inspire's debt, including amounts from its $11.3 billion acquisition of Dunkin'.

title: Trooper who helped take down Memorial Drive gunman identified as victims’ families speak out
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Trooper Landon Veney helped subdue gunman Tyler Brown during a shooting on Memorial Drive in Cambridge.
Two men, including an MBTA driver, were injured in the attack; both victims are expected to survive.
Suspect Tyler Brown faces charges including armed assault with intent to murder after firing at vehicles.
